channel.title: (Seeking Greenlight) Mirror Cyborgs
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "(Seeking Greenlight) Mirror Cyborgs" - an android doppelganger tries to assasinate the human it is a copy of

item.title: Re: (Seeking Greenlight) Mirror Cyborgs
item.content:encoded:
I guess my central concern with this is that you have a really interesting setting for the story but I'm not really sure what you're like…actually going to do with it? Like, I'm not hugely familiar with Anderson so I'll defer to Conwell in terms of how well it fits into that canon; but my concern here is just…how do you link the pieces up?

Like, the idea that for every Gyrfalcon, there's a cybernetic doppleganger hunting down the human user is fine but what are you doing with this? How does the Broken God stuff tie into this article? How do the initial pair of cyborgs tie in?

My concern is that it feels like the stuff about an assassin android attempting to hunt down a human with a robot arm is by far the most interesting part of this pitch and that nothing else really builds off that well. Maybe it'd be best writing it as a tale from the perspective of one of the two cyborgs where you can like, really explore their motivations and have cool action sequences as they nearly but don't quite get their target or w/e?

item.title: Re: (Seeking Greenlight) Mirror Cyborgs
item.content:encoded:
Good notes, I'll go back to what worked and come up with alternatives for what did not work.

But about Anderson, yes it's been stated how he uses magic, but where did that magic come from? If Methane is the god of machines, I mean not just a giant robot that claims to be a god but actually a divine entity that can actually be defined as a god, then wouldn't it be possible that Anderson's technology could fall under his domain? I was actually coming at this from a "God works in mysterious ways" approach, maybe Anderson didn't realize he was taking the power of a god when he first found his magic, but what he did still got the God's attention.

As for the plot holes around 3560, I thought about that. I believe it hasn't actually been found yet so it's claims would refer to a place that the Foundation isn't aware of yet, so it would refer to it's world in a more oblique way that makes sense to the reader but not to the in universe characters, at least not yet. Also, yes the inhabitants turn intangible when removed, but there is evidence that there are exceptions when they go hunting individuals for a reason. 2806 isn't made with the awareness that they are pulling from that dimension(Vincent and Phone as were working off studying magic without realizing the methods would summon spirits from another dimension to animate their creations or that the other dimension would come back for them), although Vincent himself suggests that tracking similar energy would help the Foundation track the instances still unaccounted for which means he may at least suspect a connection even if he's not aware. So the idea was that 2806 is animated by 3560, which is why they appear in disrepair, the parts of their souls are being borrowed to animate partial robotics. Imagine that saying "Integrate" to activate the Gyrfalcon actually summons a robot spirit to inhabit the physical construct. It would be like Taily-po, and the rest of the robot spirit is trying to get his Taily-po and is able to come get him the same way the other spirits would later come to take the Anderson traitors and thus be an exception that remains tangible because it has a specific tie to something in this world.

I thought I had this thought out, I've been researching this for two months and I thought I had this worked out. If I missed something important please provide a link. If I can't work around this, I will remove Anderson altogether and start this over as completely original, but I wanted to tie together existing lore. Every time I read a cyborg article with unclear origins I wonder who made this thing because it had to be made by someone and then Anderson and CotBG seem to cover it well and any SCP that strays makes me wonder what poor soul is making these machines and will they ever find their home in the universe with the other anomalous machines?

item.title: Re: (Seeking Greenlight) Mirror Cyborgs
item.content:encoded:
Responding to a request for crit via PM.

+ show block
– hide block

A pair of cyborgs that appear to be identical and one is trying to assassinate and replace the other. One is just a human with a Gyrfalcon series prosthetic arm, the assassin is an android(Saker, Peregrine, or something in between) with the flesh and blood arm of its target. The human knows nothing of the origin of the android, especially how it got the arm that shouldn't even still be intact. When Anderson Robotics is investigated, even they don't know where the doppelganger came from nor do they have any records of it's construction.

Alright, I actually do think this is an interesting premise so far. Well done.

The android claims to originate from SCP-3560 and implies that all Gyrfalcon prosthetics are actually powered by inhabitants of that dimension which are partially dismantled by Anderson's anomalous construction process.

I'm all for new interpretations for existing articles, but this just gets quite a few things about both SCP-2806 and SCP-3560 wrong. In addition to the incredibly detailed construction timeline in 2806 that doesn't match up with this, the fact that things from inside SCP-3560 become intangible and vanish after being removed from inside the pocket dimension would make that an incredibly poor source for such prosthetics. If you're going to make other articles so crucial to the narrative you are telling, they at a minimum should follow the logic of those SCP articles. Otherwise, why use them to begin with?

I'm currently working under the theory that the true power behind Anderson Robotics is the Broken God which has allowed Anderson to build his army, but Anderson has gotten greedy and thinks he can claim the power for himself, and his recent turn of fate is the Broken God getting even with him.

This is an interesting theory, but it's pretty clearly stated in many AR articles that the driving force behind ARs products is disguising magic as advanced tech. The current turn of fate is due to his greed, sure, but not related to any divine intervention.

As I said before, I'm all for new interpretations of older articles, but if doing so overlooks the fundamental mechanics of the original article then why use it to begin with as opposed to creating something new that you have all the freedom to tool around with.

I wish you luck, but this has a few too many plot holes at the moment for me to greenlight.

item.title: (Seeking Greenlight) Mirror Cyborgs
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Science Fiction

Page Layout: Standard SCP article

Elevator Pitch: A pair of cyborgs that appear to be identical and one is trying to assassinate and replace the other. One is just a human with a Gyrfalcon series prosthetic arm, the assassin is an android(Saker, Peregrine, or something in between) with the flesh and blood arm of its target. The human knows nothing of the origin of the android, especially how it got the arm that shouldn't even still be intact. When Anderson Robotics is investigated, even they don't know where the doppelganger came from nor do they have any records of it's construction.

Central Narrative:

+ show block
– hide block

The android claims to originate from SCP-3560 and implies that all Gyrfalcon prosthetics are actually powered by inhabitants of that dimension which are partially dismantled by Anderson's anomalous construction process.

This android regenerated the limb from it's human counterpart, though how remains a mystery, and now it's hunting for the rest of the body. But it definitely must be contained for futher interrogation and to prevent it from killing it's target.

The target has proven to be relatively innocent and agreed to work for the Foundation as a double agent in the Church of Maxwell. Human is temporarily contained in human containment cell pending review for possible recruitment to the Foundation.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: For every Gyrfalcon, there is a cybernetic doppelganger hunting down the human user, this is just the only one they found so far.

+ show block
– hide block

Additional Notes: I'm currently working under the theory that the true power behind Anderson Robotics is the Broken God which has allowed Anderson to build his army, but Anderson has gotten greedy and thinks he can claim the power for himself, and his recent turn of fate is the Broken God getting even with him. SCP-3560 was mentioned to have predated Anderson in a footnote in SCP-4000, I am assuming that rather than simply being an afterlife for the robots, it's more of in between lives, that they existed before and every time Anderson creates a new robot, it's a copy of an existing soul and the all the tech has been reverse engineered from the ancient army of Wan.
